blurb:
Jacquelyn came in from animal control, landed in a foster home, and promptly made herself at home with three other dogs. She's about two years old, 58 pounds, and her foster describes her as lovable, silly, and sweet. She's also a velcro dog, meaning she will find a way to be wherever you are and get her fair share of the affection. She's learned to dial it back when asked, though, so she's not chaos, just enthusiastic.

She does have a congenital kidney defect, which sounds scarier than it is. Her foster says it's easily managed and hasn't slowed her down at all. She just needs a few potty breaks during the day, no more than a typical dog, and she's been solid through the night. She's living fully and just wants someone to do that with.

She'd do best in a home where someone is around during the day and would love a friendly dog companion. Cats aren't her thing, and she hasn't been around toddlers, so meet-and-greet recommended if you have little ones. Mostly Mutts is in Kennesaw. Head to their website to start an application and get a visit scheduled.
shelter_hours: Please check mostlymutts.org for current hours
scoring_notes:
• A managed medical condition paired with a glowing foster report reframes a potential barrier as a compelling underdog story
• 'Living fully despite the odds' is the kind of narrative that converts casual readers into applicants
• Came in from animal control with no prior backstory, and household restrictions likely extend her wait, adding quiet urgency
